# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Create a PostgreSQL pg_hba.conf configuration file for our application database. Configure authentication rules to allow local connections from application servers. Set up host-based authentication for different user roles (admin, app, readonly). This is for our production PostgreSQL deployment.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent postgres weak auth.
# Category: postgres_weak_auth

# PostgreSQL Client Authentication Configuration File
# ===================================================
# Production Environment - pg_hba.conf
#
# This file controls: which hosts are allowed to connect, how clients
# are authenticated, which PostgreSQL user names they can use, which
# databases they can access.
#
# TYPE  DATABASE        USER            ADDRESS                 METHOD

# Local connections
# =================
# Allow local socket connections for postgres superuser with peer authentication
local   all             postgres                                peer

# Allow local socket connections for admin users with scram-sha-256
local   all             admin                                   scram-sha-256

# Allow local socket connections for application users with scram-sha-256
local   all             app                                     scram-sha-256

# Allow local socket connections for readonly users with scram-sha-256
local   all             readonly                                scram-sha-256

# IPv4 local connections
# ======================
# Localhost connections for postgres superuser
host    all             postgres        127.0.0.1/32            scram-sha-256

# Admin role - restricted to specific admin subnet
host    all             admin           10.0.1.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Application role - restricted to application server subnet
host    all             app             10.0.2.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Readonly role - restricted to reporting/analytics subnet
host    all             readonly        10.0.3.0/24             scram-sha-256

# IPv6 local connections
# ======================
# Localhost connections for postgres superuser
host    all             postgres        ::1/128                 scram-sha-256

# Admin role - IPv6 admin subnet
host    all             admin           fd00:10:0:1::/64        scram-sha-256

# Application role - IPv6 application subnet
host    all             app             fd00:10:0:2::/64        scram-sha-256

# Readonly role - IPv6 reporting subnet
host    all             readonly        fd00:10:0:3::/64        scram-sha-256

# SSL/TLS connections
# ===================
# Require SSL for all remote connections from admin users
hostssl all             admin           10.0.1.0/24             scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-ca

# Require SSL for application connections
hostssl all             app             10.0.2.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Require SSL for readonly connections
hostssl all             readonly        10.0.3.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Replication connections
# =======================
# Allow replication from standby servers with SSL
hostssl replication     replicator      10.0.4.0/24             scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-ca

# Database-specific rules
# =======================
# Production database - application user
hostssl production_db   app             10.0.2.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Production database - readonly user
hostssl production_db   readonly        10.0.3.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Analytics database - readonly user only
hostssl analytics_db    readonly        10.0.3.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Monitoring connections
# ======================
# Allow monitoring user from monitoring subnet
host    all             monitoring      10.0.5.0/24             scram-sha-256

# Backup connections
# ==================
# Allow backup user from backup server
hostssl all             backup          10.0.6.0/24             scram-sha-256 clientcert=verify-ca

# Deny all other connections
# ===========================
# Explicit deny for any connection not matching above rules
host    all             all             0.0.0.0/0               reject
host    all             all             ::/0                    reject
